_meta.author: scorecard-refine-national autopilot
_meta.note: Evidence-finalize national federal-House batch (5): two real challengers scored from their own cited statements/record (arthur-ellis MD-05, melissa-strange KY-04), and three synthetic open-seat placeholders RETIRED after currency research showed no single scoreable nominee exists (ks-03: incumbent Davids actually running for reelection — placeholder premise false; la-05: Letlow vacated to run for Senate, GOP primary SUSPENDED pending court-ordered map redraw, contested field no nominee; ma-06: Moulton vacated to run for Senate, contested 7-way Dem primary 9/1/2026, no nominee). Placeholders set status:former / not_running / roster_verified with the real field documented and a revisit date — never scored as a person. Every TRUE/FALSE cites a source; nulls = no evidence found; never inferred from party.

arthur-ellis.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06-16. Active 2026 Democratic candidate for the open MD-05 (Hoyer retired; Hoyer NOT running). Crowded ~24-way Dem primary on 2026-06-23 (not yet held); Ellis polled mid-pack (~5%), no presumptive nominee. Sitting Maryland State Senator (District 28, Charles Co., since 2019); Air Force veteran; CPA. Record/positions are conventionally liberal with ONE hard cited fact for our rubric: named primary co-sponsor of Maryland SB1, the 2023 Gun Safety Act (restricts lawful concealed carry locations even with a permit), passed Senate 31-16 — an affirmative pro-gun-restriction record (scored self_defense). Campaign positions otherwise vague/soft: 'protect maternal health,' 'humane immigration reform,' universal pre-K, 'end gerrymandering' (he staged a one-man floor protest demanding a vote on a mid-decade Dem-favorable redistricting bill) — none specific enough to score, left null. NO cross-party (conservative) deviation found. Religion: no public faith affiliation stated.

0.note: Named primary co-sponsor of Maryland SB1, the 2023 Gun Safety Act, which restricts where a permit-holder may lawfully carry a concealed firearm (passed Senate 31-16) — documented contrary to permitless/expanded concealed carry.

2.note: Co-sponsoring SB1 is an affirmative act expanding gun regulation — documented contrary to firearm deregulation (NFA/GCA repeal).

melissa-strange.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06-16. CONFIRMED Democratic nominee for KY-04: won the 2026-05-19 Dem primary; faces Republican Ed Gallrein (Trump-endorsed former Navy SEAL who ousted incumbent Rep. Thomas Massie in the GOP primary) in the deep-red (R+~18) Nov 2026 general — a self-described long-shot. First-time candidate, no prior office or voting record. Director of Supply Chain Management (ADM); NKU/Thomas More grad. Scored from her OWN campaign Issues page: explicitly pro-choice ('Personal medical decisions should remain between patients and their healthcare providers—not politicians'; pledges to safeguard/expand reproductive, maternal and newborn care 'including access to IVF, birth control') — scored sanctity_of_life. All other planks are soft slogans (healthcare access, affordability/jobs, Pre-K/public-education funding, generic 'Equality/respect for everyone') with no citable specific position on marriage, guns, immigration, elections, religious liberty, economy, or foreign policy — left null. No single-issue-group endorsement (Planned Parenthood/NRA/Giffords/HRC) could be confirmed. NO cross-party (conservative) deviation found. Religion: not publicly stated.

0.note: Own campaign Issues page states personal medical decisions should be 'between patients and their healthcare providers—not politicians' and lists 'reproductive health and rights' as a core platform — a clear self-stated pro-choice position.

2.note: Campaign pledges to 'safeguard and expand' reproductive/maternal healthcare 'including access to IVF, birth control' — her own stated position to expand IVF access, documented contrary to the embryo-protection position.

set.office: U.S. Representative KS-03 (synthetic placeholder retired — seat NOT open; incumbent Davids running for reelection)

ks-03-challenger-placeholder.notes_set: RETIRED 2026-06-16 (currency research). This was a synthetic 'generic R challenger' placeholder built on a FALSE premise ('open seat · Davids seat'): KS-03 is NOT open — incumbent Democrat Sharice Davids filed for reelection on 2026-05-11 (see the sharice-davids record). The actual 2026 Republican field is a CONTESTED multi-candidate primary (Aug 4, 2026, not yet held) — Chase LaPorte, Eric Jenkins, and Blake Stanley — with NO presumptive nominee, so no real person can be evidence-scored here. Placeholder set status:former/not_running so it leaves the active scoring queue; all cells null (never scored a nonexistent person). REVISIT after the 2026-08-04 KS primary to create a real record for the winning GOP nominee vs. Davids.

set.office: U.S. Representative LA-05 (synthetic placeholder retired — open seat, GOP primary suspended, no nominee)

la-05-r-placeholder.notes_set: RETIRED 2026-06-16 (currency research). Rep. Julia Letlow (R) vacated LA-05 to run for U.S. Senate (announced 2026-01-20, advanced to a 6/27 GOP Senate runoff) — see the julia-letlow record, already tagged running_higher_office. The LA-05 House seat is OPEN and CONTESTED with no nominee: the GOP primary (Louisiana adopted closed party primaries for 2026 congressional races) was originally 5/16/2026 but is SUSPENDED by Executive Order 26-038 (4/30/2026) pending a court-ordered congressional map redraw after a SCOTUS ruling. Declared R candidates: Blake Miguez (state senator, Trump-endorsed), Rick Edmonds (state senator), Michael Echols (state rep), Misti Cordell (Board of Regents chair) + minor candidates — a Feb poll had the top three 'virtually tied in single digits' with ~75% undecided. No single scoreable nominee. Placeholder set status:former/not_running; all cells null. REVISIT once the suspended primary resolves and a nominee emerges.

set.office: U.S. Representative MA-06 (synthetic placeholder retired — open seat, contested 7-way D primary, no nominee)

ma-06-d-placeholder.notes_set: RETIRED 2026-06-16 (currency research). Rep. Seth Moulton (D) vacated MA-06 to run for U.S. Senate against Ed Markey (announced 2025-10-15). The seat is OPEN with a CONTESTED 7-way Democratic primary on 2026-09-01 (not yet held) and NO presumptive nominee: Dan Koh, John Beccia, Jamie Belsito, Tram Nguyen, Beth Andres-Beck, Mariah Lancaster, Rachel Creemers (Rick Jakious withdrew 4/15/2026). Koh leads in money only; a May poll showed 59% undecided with no durable leader. No single scoreable person. Placeholder set status:former/not_running; all cells null. REVISIT after the 2026-09-01 primary to create a real record for the Democratic nominee.
